relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Moses, Aaron, sons, and elders are called upward to worship from afar.
Moses alone draws near while people remain below.
The people answer with one voice: all YHWH spoke, we will do.
Words are written, altar built, and twelve stones rise for the tribes.
Young ones offer burnt and peace offerings.
Blood/life is divided between basins and altar.
The scroll is read, and the people say: we will do and we will hear.
Covenant blood/life is sprinkled as the words bind people and YHWH.
Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders ascend.
They see the Divine of Israel above sapphire purity.
The nobles behold Divine and eat and drink.
Moses is called up for stone tablets and teaching.
Moses rises with Joshua toward the mountain of Divine.
Aaron and Hur remain for the people's matters.
Glory dwells six days, and the seventh day calls Moses from cloud.
The glory appears as consuming fire before Israel.
Moses enters the cloud for forty days and forty nights.

contemplative_note: Exodus 24 is covenant sealed and seen: words written, life sprinkled, elders eating before Divine, sapphire underfoot, cloud above, and Moses entering glory.
